title: How to get a police certificate - Sudan - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate - Sudan
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Certificate of Good Conduct ( Feesh )

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.
Apply in person at the Ministry of Interior, General Administration of Forensic Evidence, Fingerprint Directorate (see “ Contact information ” for the complete address).
Your fingerprints will be recorded on the front and the results will be recorded on the back.
- Have a complete set of your fingerprints taken by the local police where you live. The police must sign and stamp them.
- The police must sign and stamp them.
- Take your completed fingerprints to the nearest Sudanese embassy.
- The embassy will send your fingerprints to the Ministry of Interior, General Administration of Forensic Evidence, Fingerprint Directorate on your behalf.

## Documents you will need
- Personal identification or passport
- Passport-size photograph
Ministry of Interior General Administration of Forensic Evidence Fingerprint Directorate
Nile Street, Khartoum
Sudan Phone: +249 18 377 5300
